methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $117M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 6 same-sector + same-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $120M - $5.0B.

data.narrative:
VEIR, a Series B climate and energy technology company that has raised $117M to date, was valued using two triangulation methods against a peer set of 6 same-stage climate and energy companies. The comparable universe spans 1 public company (LNZA) and 8 private entities, including Commonwealth Fusion Systems, Pano AI, Sage Geosystems, Reverion, and CarbonCapture Inc.

Stage-progression methodology, applying a 1.9x step-up multiplier typical of Series B advancement, yields $222M. Peer-median analysis across the 6-company comparable set produces $355M, with individual peer valuations ranging from $120M to $5.0B. The peer median reflects a broad dispersion driven by Commonwealth Fusion Systems' outlier $5.0B valuation; the interquartile range of $310M–$500M offers a tighter band.

Triangulation across these two methods supports a valuation range of $189M (low), $289M (median), and $408M (high). The gap between stage-progression and peer-median reflects uncertainty around capital efficiency and market timing relative to comparable exits. Key sensitivities include VEIR's relative technology maturity versus peers, customer pipeline depth, and sector funding environment volatility. The $120M–$500M peer band (excluding the Commonwealth outlier) may better represent normalized market pricing for this stage.
